full_response:
To write down the product of two square matrices [M] and [N] using the Σ sum notation, we first define the matrices.

Let [M] and [N] be 3x3 matrices as indicated by the entries listed in the problem:
[M] =
$
\begin{pmatrix}
M_{11} & M_{12} & M_{13} \\
M_{21} & M_{22} & M_{23} \\
M_{31} & M_{32} & M_{33}
\end{pmatrix}
$

[N] =
$
\begin{pmatrix}
N_{11} & N_{12} & N_{13} \\
N_{21} & N_{22} & N_{23} \\
N_{31} & N_{32} & N_{33}
\end{pmatrix}
$

Let [P] be the product matrix [M]·[N]. The element in the i-th row and j-th column of [P], denoted as P_{ij}, is obtained by taking the dot product of the i-th row of [M] and the j-th column of [N].

In general, for matrices where [M] is an m x n matrix and [N] is an n x p matrix, the product [P] = [M]·[N] is an m x p matrix, and its elements P_{ij} are given by the sum:
$ P_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{n} M_{ik} N_{kj} $

In this specific problem, both [M] and [N] are 3x3 matrices. Thus, m=3, n=3, and p=3.
Therefore, an element P_{ij} of the product matrix [P] = [M]·[N] is given by:
$ P_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{ik} N_{kj} $

To explicitly "write down [M]·[N]" using this notation, we form the product matrix [P] by defining each of its elements using the sum notation:

$ [M]·[N] = [P] = $
$
\begin{pmatrix}
\sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{1k} N_{k1} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{1k} N_{k2} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{1k} N_{k3} \\
\sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{2k} N_{k1} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{2k} N_{k2} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{2k} N_{k3} \\
\sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{3k} N_{k1} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{3k} N_{k2} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{3k} N_{k3}
\end{pmatrix}
$

Where the summation index $k$ runs from 1 to 3.

The problem also mentions a row vector v and a column vector u, but it does not ask for any operations involving them, only for the matrix multiplication [M]·[N].

The product [M]·[N] is a matrix [P] where the entry in the i-th row and j-th column is given by $P_{ij} = \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{ik} N_{kj}$.
$$
\boxed{
[M]·[N] = 
\begin{pmatrix}
\sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{1k} N_{k1} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{1k} N_{k2} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{1k} N_{k3} \\
\sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{2k} N_{k1} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{2k} N_{k2} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{2k} N_{k3} \\
\sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{3k} N_{k1} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{3k} N_{k2} & \sum_{k=1}^{3} M_{3k} N_{k3}
\end{pmatrix}
}
$$
Confidence: 100%
